tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine and chloride, low urine specific gravity, significant proteinuria, microalbuminuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function and possible nephropathy. Urine glucose and ketones are present, but blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, which may indicate a non-diabetic cause. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying renal pathology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
